soap:
RE-REVIEW · lance-format/lance#7464 — perf(index): coalesce concurrent scalar-index opens (single-flight)
reviewed 7fef56a1 → 8ab25dec (47 commits on branch; 5 of the PR's own files in delta, 136 base-merge files excluded)

## Subjective

Author: Ali2Arslan. This is the re-review pass. My single prior blocking condition asked the author to route cold scalar-index opens through a new `ScalarIndexPlugin::get_or_insert_in_cache()` rather than the raw unsized `get_from_cache`/`put_in_cache` dance, so that plugins downcast `Arc<dyn ScalarIndex>` into a sized state before caching — the precondition for `CacheCodec` correctness and for retiring the `*_unsized` cache access variants. The author reports the thread resolved. CI is green and the PR is mergeable per the live packet build.

## Objective

Delta scoped to the PR's own work touches 5 files: `registry.rs`, `bitmap.rs`, `btree.rs`, `label_list.rs`, and `rust/lance/src/index/scalar.rs`.

Condition #1 (blocking) — add `ScalarIndexPlugin::get_or_insert_in_cache()`:
- `registry.rs` adds the trait method `get_or_insert_in_cache(index_store, frag_reuse_index, cache, load: ScalarIndexLoad)` with a documented non-coalescing default (read-through/write-back over `get_from_cache`/`put_in_cache`), plus a new `single_flight_open<K, ToState, FromState>` free helper and the `ScalarIndexLoad<'a> = BoxFuture<'a, Result<Arc<dyn ScalarIndex>>>` alias.
- All three serializable plugins override it with `single_flight_open`, wiring their sized state key (`BitmapIndexStateKey` / `BTreeIndexStateKey` / `LabelListIndexStateKey`), a `to_state` downcast (`from_scalar_index` / `from_index`), and an IO-free `from_state` reconstruct.
- Call site: the caller in `rust/lance/src/index/scalar.rs` now invokes `plugin.get_or_insert_in_cache(...)` in place of the manual `get_from_cache` → `load` → `put_in_cache` sequence (confirmed against the file at head; the compare showed a spurious 0/0 patch for this path, but the head content invokes the new method).

The downcast-before-cache shape the condition asked for is present: `to_state: FnOnce(&dyn ScalarIndex) -> Result<K::ValueType>` forces the `Arc<dyn ScalarIndex>` → sized-state conversion at cache-put time, and `K::ValueType: DeepSizeOf`, so the codec sees a sized value. This is exactly the mechanism the condition required.

Beyond the condition, the delta carries two changes not described by the PR title:
1. `FragReuseIndex` → `Arc<dyn RowIdRemapper>` trait-object migration threaded through every plugin signature and the `ScalarIndexPlugin` trait (`load_index`, `get_from_cache`, `to_bitmap_index`, `reconstruct`, `into_label_list_index`, `remap_row_ids`, etc.).
2. `label_list.rs` removes the `fragment_ids.is_some()` guard that previously errored `"LabelList index does not support fragment training"`, and adds a new 164-line public `merge_label_list_indices(...)`.

## Assessment

The blocking condition is **met**. The plugin-level `get_or_insert_in_cache` exists, downcasts to a sized state before caching via `single_flight_open`, and the caller uses it. This is the correct shape for `CacheCodec` and for retiring the unsized access variants.

The coalescing claim — now **verified** (was my one blocking-hinge question): `single_flight_open` does no locking itself; it delegates to `cache.get_or_insert_with_key` → `CacheBackend::get_or_insert` → moka's `optionally_get_with` (`rust/lance-core/src/cache/moka.rs:74-112`), a genuine per-key single-flight primitive (same class as `try_get_with`/`get_with`) — the init future runs at most once per key under concurrent access, with no check-then-insert race. On a failing `load`, moka's `init` returns `None`, so the error is propagated and **not** cached/poisoned; the next caller retries — correct for a fallible-IO loader. This whole cache path (mod.rs:366, the moka backend) is pre-existing and unchanged on the base SHA; the PR's only `cache/mod.rs` change is a deletion (−103: it retires `get_or_insert_unsized_with_key`), moving the single-flight coalescing from the unsized `Arc<dyn Trait>` key onto the sized serializable state. The perf claim in the PR title is therefore earned.

One residual concern remains:

- **Undescribed feature in `label_list.rs` (fresh-eyes).** Removing the fragment-training guard and adding `merge_label_list_indices` (164 lines) enables distributed/segment LabelList builds — a feature change unrelated to "coalesce concurrent scalar-index opens." **Verified it is NOT dead code:** `merge_label_list_indices` (label_list.rs:500) is called by `merge_segments` (`rust/lance/src/index/scalar/label_list.rs:128`), dispatched from `merge_existing_index_segments` (`rust/lance/src/index.rs:1187`, the `all_label_list` arm, exactly parallel to the bitmap sibling at :1186). The `train_index` fragment-training guard is confirmed removed and the comment now ties fragment-scoped training to `merge_label_list_indices`. So this is a real, wired feature — but it's undescribed by the PR title and inflates the review surface of a perf change. It deserves its own PR/description and its own merge + fragment-training tests, or an explicit justification for bundling it here.

Also verified (fresh-eyes): the `FragReuseIndex` → `Arc<dyn RowIdRemapper>` trait-object migration (new trait at `scalar.rs:1141`, impl at `frag_reuse.rs:25`) is **complete and coherent** — 13 scalar-index files migrated, zero newly-added `Arc<FragReuseIndex>` parameter sites remain; the concrete type only appears in its own definition/impl. No concern there.

Minor: the downcast helper is named `from_index` in btree but `from_scalar_index` in bitmap/label_list — harmless, but worth unifying.

## Plan

Blocking condition met; single-flight verified; migration coherent. The one remaining item is process/scope, not a defect — so this is a **comment**, not a block.

RECOMMENDATION: comment

- Justify or split the `label_list.rs` changes: `merge_label_list_indices` IS wired (into the segment-merge dispatch), so it's a real feature — but it's undescribed by this perf PR and lacks its own merge / fragment-training tests. Either split it out or document + test it here.
- (nit) Unify the `from_index` / `from_scalar_index` downcast-helper naming.

confidence: high. The condition is verified at trait, plugin, and call site; the single-flight perf claim is verified end-to-end (moka `optionally_get_with`, no check-then-insert, errors not poisoned); the `RowIdRemapper` migration is complete. The only judgment call is whether to accept the bundled LabelList feature — a scope/process comment, not a blocker.
summary: Prior blocking condition is met: `ScalarIndexPlugin::get_or_insert_in_cache()` now exists (registry.rs), downcasts `Arc<dyn ScalarIndex>` to a sized state before caching via the new `single_flight_open` helper in all three serializable plugins, and the caller in rust/lance/src/index/scalar.rs invokes it. Verified end-to-end: `single_flight_open` delegates to moka's `optionally_get_with` (cache/moka.rs), a true per-key single-flight primitive with no check-then-insert race, and failing loads are not poisoned — the perf claim is earned. Fresh-eyes: label_list.rs removes the fragment-training guard and adds a 164-line `merge_label_list_indices` — verified NOT dead code (wired into merge_existing_index_segments at index.rs:1187), but it's an undescribed feature bundled into a perf PR and lacks its own merge/fragment-training tests. The FragReuseIndex -> Arc<dyn RowIdRemapper> migration is complete and coherent. Recommend comment: condition satisfied and single-flight verified, but split/justify + test the LabelList feature before merge.

comments.label: issue
comments.body: **issue (non-blocking):** This 164-line `merge_label_list_indices` plus the removal of the `fragment_ids.is_some()` "LabelList index does not support fragment training" guard enable distributed/segment LabelList builds — a feature change unrelated to the PR's stated scope (coalescing concurrent scalar-index opens). I confirmed it's not dead code: it's wired into `merge_existing_index_segments` (rust/lance/src/index.rs:1187, the `all_label_list` arm parallel to the bitmap sibling) via `merge_segments`. But that makes it an undescribed feature riding in a perf PR, and I don't see dedicated tests for the new merge path or for fragment-scoped LabelList training. Please either split it into its own PR/description or, if it stays, document it and add merge + fragment-training tests.

comments.label: nitpick
comments.body: **nitpick:** The sized-state downcast helper is `from_index` here but `from_scalar_index` in bitmap.rs and label_list.rs. Same role in all three — worth unifying the name for grep-ability.
